policy.title: Privacy Policy
policy.section1_desc: BarNote(이하 '회사')는 다음의 목적을 위하여 개인정보를 처리합니다. 처리하고 있는 개인정보는 다음의 목적 이외의 용도로는 이용되지 않으며, 이용 목적이 변경되는 경우에는 사전에 동의를 구합니다.
policy.s1_item1: 회원 가입 의사 확인 및 회원제 서비스 제공
policy.s1_item2: 시음 노트 및 취향 정보 분석, 맞춤형 서비스 제공
policy.s1_item3: 고객 문의 대응 및 민원 처리
policy.section2_desc: 회사는 원활한 서비스 제공을 위해 다음의 개인정보를 수집하고 있습니다.
policy.s2_item1: 필수항목: 이메일, 비밀번호, 닉네임, 기기 정보
policy.s2_item2: 선택항목: 프로필 사진, 한 줄 소개
policy.s2_item3: 서비스 이용 기록: 접속 로그, 접속 IP 정보, 시음 노트, 팔로우 등
policy.section3_desc: 원칙적으로 개인정보 처리목적이 달성된 후에는 해당 정보를 지체 없이 파기합니다. (단, 관련 법령에 의해 보존할 필요가 있는 경우 해당 법령에서 정한 기간 동안 보존)
policy.section4_desc: 이용자는 등록되어 있는 본인의 개인정보를 조회하거나 수정할 수 있으며, 동의 철회 및 가입 해지를 요청할 수 있습니다.
policy.section5_desc: 개인정보 보호 관련 문의, 불만 처리, 피해 구제 등에 관한 사항은 아래 연락처로 문의하시기 바랍니다.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.section1_desc: These Terms of Service govern the rights, obligations, and responsibilities between you and BarNote ("Company") regarding the use of the BarNote service. This End User License Agreement (EULA) also applies when you download and use our app through global app stores (App Store, Google Play, etc.).
terms.section2_desc: The key terms used in these Terms of Service are defined as follows:
terms.s2_item1: 'User' refers to a member who receives services provided by the Company according to these Terms.
terms.s2_item2: 'Member' refers to a person who has registered for the service and can use the services provided by the Company.
terms.s2_item3: 'Content' refers to signs, texts, figures, colors, voices, sounds, and images (tasting notes and photos) posted by members within the service.
terms.section3_desc: The Company may revise these Terms within the scope that does not violate relevant laws if a reasonable cause arises. Revised Terms will be announced through notices within the service and take effect from the announced effective date.
terms.section4_desc: Users shall not engage in any of the following acts when using the service. Violations may result in restricted service use or account deletion.
terms.s4_item1: Registering or acting by stealing someone else's information or providing false information.
terms.s4_item2: Infringing upon the copyrights or other intellectual property rights of the Company or third parties.
terms.s4_item3: Damaging the reputation or disrupting the business of the Company and third parties.
terms.s4_item4: Other illegal or unfair acts such as distributing spam or malicious code.
terms.section5_title: Article 5 (Zero Tolerance Policy & EULA on Objectionable Content)
terms.section5_desc: As the Company provides a service allowing users to freely write and share tasting notes, we enforce a strict Zero Tolerance Policy against Objectionable Content and Abusive Users.
terms.s5_item1: Definition of Objectionable Content: Includes hate speech, sexually explicit or obscene content, harassing or threatening content, content promoting violence, and content encouraging illegal activities.
terms.s5_item2: Proactive Blocking and Deletion: If objectionable content is discovered or reported within the service, the Company may completely delete or hide the content (tasting notes, profiles, etc.) immediately without prior notice.
terms.s5_item3: User Sanctions: Abusive users who post objectionable content or cause discomfort to others will have their accounts blocked immediately without warning, or their use of the service will be permanently restricted.
terms.s5_item4: Reporting and Response Mechanism: Users can report objectionable content or abusive users at any time using the in-app reporting feature. The Company will review the report immediately upon receipt and take appropriate corrective actions within 24 hours.
terms.section6_desc: The Company shall not be held liable for providing the service if it is unable to do so due to force majeure events such as natural disasters, service maintenance, or user's fault. Furthermore, unless otherwise specified by relevant laws, the Company is not responsible for any damages related to the use of legally free services.
terms.section7_desc: In the event of a dispute related to the use of the service, it shall be resolved amicably according to the principle of good faith. If it cannot be resolved, a lawsuit may be filed in the competent court. The competent court shall be the court with exclusive jurisdiction over the location of the Company's headquarters.
